title: Potential Race Condition in Ingestion Command
description: The ingestion command does not handle concurrent executions properly, which may lead to race conditions when multiple ingestion jobs are run simultaneously.

root_cause_potential: Ingestion command lacks concurrency control mechanisms
Multiple simultaneous ingestion jobs can interfere with each other
Shared resources accessed without proper locking
No job queue system to serialize ingestion operations
technical_analysis.suggested_solution: Implement locking mechanisms or use a job queue to manage concurrent executions

reproduction_steps: 2. Observe potential conflicts in file processing
3. Check for data corruption or incomplete ingestion
4. Monitor system logs for race condition indicators
expected_behavior: Ingestion jobs should execute safely in parallel or be serialized through a proper queue system
actual_behavior: Multiple concurrent ingestion jobs may interfere with each other causing data corruption

immediate_fixes.action: Implement file-level locking for ingestion operations
immediate_fixes.rationale: Prevent simultaneous access to same resources during ingestion

immediate_fixes.action: Add job queue system for ingestion pipeline
immediate_fixes.rationale: Serialize ingestion operations to prevent race conditions

immediate_fixes.action: Add concurrency safety checks in ingestion command
immediate_fixes.rationale: Detect and prevent unsafe concurrent operations

impact_assessment.user_impact: High - Data corruption risk during bulk ingestion
impact_assessment.functionality_impact: Critical - Affects core ingestion pipeline reliability
impact_assessment.urgency: High - Must be resolved before production deployment
